1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9	)
10	
11	// SuccessStatus mirrors agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess. The agent package's
12	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guard catches drift if either side
13	// renames. Defined locally so escalation does not import agent.
14	const SuccessStatus = "success"
15	
16	// EscalatableStatuses is the set of executor status strings that warrant
17	// retrying with a higher tier. Mirrors a subset of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*
18	// strings; the agent package has a TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab
19	// guard (see agent/tier_escalation_alignment_test.go) to catch drift.
20	var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
21		"execution_failed":      true,
22		"post_run_check_failed": true,
23		"land_conflict":         true,
24	}
25	
26	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
27	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
28	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
29	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
30		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
31	}
32	
33	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
34	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
35	// locking out a provider for too long.
36	//
37	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
38	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
39	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
40	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
41	
42	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
43	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
44		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
45		if probeResult != "" {
46			body += " probe=" + probeResult
47		}
48		if detail != "" {
49			body += "\n" + detail
50		}
51		return body
52	}
53	
54	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
55	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
56	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
57	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
58	
59	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
60	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
61	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
62	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
63	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
64		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
65		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
66		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
67		Status     string  `json:"status"`
68		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
69		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
70	}
71	
72	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
73	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
74	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
75	type EscalationSummary struct {
76		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
77		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
78		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
79		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
80	}
81	
82	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
83	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
84	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
85	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
86	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
87	// SuccessStatus.
88	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
89		out := EscalationSummary{
90			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
91			WinningTier:    winningTier,
92		}
93		if out.WinningTier == "" {
94			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
95		}
96		for _, a := range attempts {
97			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
98			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
99				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
100			}
101		}
102		return out
103	}
104	
105	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
106	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
107	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
108	type BeadEventAppender interface {
109		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
110	}
111	
112	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
113	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
114	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
115	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
116	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
117	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
118		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
119			return nil
120		}
121		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
122		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
123		if err != nil {
124			return err
125		}
126		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
127			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
128			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
129			Body:      string(body),
130			Actor:     actor,
131			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
132			CreatedAt: createdAt,
133		})
134	}

3	import (

13	)
14	
15	// CandidateResult is the outcome of a single ImplementationPass inside a live
16	// worktree. The coordinator owns the worktree lifetime; callers must not remove
17	// WorktreePath directly.
18	type CandidateResult struct {
19		// Report is the full execute-bead report from the implementation pass.
20		Report ExecuteBeadReport
21		// WorktreePath is the live worktree path, valid until the coordinator
22		// disposes it after reaching terminal disposition.
23		WorktreePath string
24		// CycleIndex is the zero-based repair-cycle index for this candidate.
25		CycleIndex int
26	}
27	
28	// CandidateCycleState is the shared on-disk snapshot of the candidate-cycle
29	// phase currently owning a live attempt worktree.
30	type CandidateCycleState struct {
31		Active       bool
32		Phase        string
33		CandidateRef string
34		CandidateRev string
35		CycleIndex   int
36		ReviewActive bool
37		RepairActive bool
38	}
39	
40	// ImplementationPass runs the agent in an isolated worktree and returns a
41	// CandidateResult. It does not own terminal cleanup — the AttemptCycleCoordinator
42	// is responsible for worktree removal after disposition.
43	type ImplementationPass interface {
44		Execute(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (CandidateResult, error)
45	}
46	
47	// CandidateRefStore pins and unpins candidate git refs while the coordinator
48	// evaluates them. Refs survive executor cleanup so the landing gate can check
49	// out the candidate commit without racing concurrent garbage collection.
50	//
51	// Ref naming convention: refs/ddx/iterations/<attemptID>/<cycleIndex>
52	type CandidateRefStore interface {
53		PinCandidateRef(projectRoot, attemptID string, cycleIndex int, rev string) (ref string, err error)
54		UnpinCandidateRef(projectRoot, ref string) error
55	}
56	
57	// CandidateCheckResult carries the outcome of a pre-land quality check.
58	type CandidateCheckResult struct {
59		Passed    bool
60		Detail    string
61		Artifacts []string
62	}
63	
64	// CandidateCheckRunner runs pre-land quality checks against a candidate.
65	type CandidateCheckRunner interface {
66		RunChecks(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, candidate CandidateResult) (CandidateCheckResult, error)
67	}
68	
69	// CandidateReviewResult carries a reviewer verdict for a committed candidate.
70	type CandidateReviewResult struct {
71		// Verdict is one of "APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES", or "BLOCK".
72		Verdict              string
73		Rationale            string
74		PerAC                []ReviewAC
75		Findings             []Finding
76		VerificationCommands []string
77		Classification       string
78	}
79	
80	// CandidateReviewer runs a read-only review pass against a committed candidate
81	// inside the still-live worktree. Reserved for future candidate-cycle review
82	// implementation (non-scope for FEAT-010 initial pass).
83	type CandidateReviewer interface {
84		Review(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, candidate CandidateResult) (CandidateReviewResult, error)
85	}
86	
87	// RepairPass attempts to repair a candidate in the still-live worktree based on
88	// the repair prompt, returning an updated CandidateResult with an append-only
89	// repair commit.
90	type RepairPass interface {
91		Repair(ctx context.Context, candidate CandidateResult, prompt string) (CandidateResult, error)
92	}
93	
94	// CandidateLander lands an approved candidate to the main branch.
95	type CandidateLander interface {
96		Land(ctx context.Context, candidate CandidateResult) (ExecuteBeadReport, error)
97	}
98	
99	// candidateIterationRef returns the ref name for a pinned candidate cycle.
100	// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<attemptID>/<cycleIndex>
101	func candidateIterationRef(attemptID string, cycleIndex int) string {
102		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%d", attemptID, cycleIndex)
103	}
104	
105	// GitCandidateRefStore is the production CandidateRefStore implementation.
106	// It pins and unpins candidate refs in the project root via git update-ref so
107	// they remain accessible after the temporary implementation worktree is removed.
108	type GitCandidateRefStore struct{}
109	
110	func (s *GitCandidateRefStore) PinCandidateRef(projectRoot, attemptID string, cycleIndex int, rev string) (string, error) {
111		ref := candidateIterationRef(attemptID, cycleIndex)
112		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "update-ref", ref, rev).CombinedOutput()
113		if err != nil {
114			return "", fmt.Errorf("pin candidate ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
115		}
116		return ref, nil
117	}
118	
119	func (s *GitCandidateRefStore) UnpinCandidateRef(projectRoot, ref string) error {
120		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), projectRoot, "update-ref", "-d", ref).CombinedOutput()
121		if err != nil {
122			return fmt.Errorf("unpin candidate ref %s: %s: %w", ref, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
123		}
124		return nil
125	}
126	
127	// CandidateCycleEventBody is the structured payload of a candidate_cycle_pinned bead event.
128	type CandidateCycleEventBody struct {
129		CandidateRef string `json:"candidate_ref"`
130		CycleIndex   int    `json:"cycle_index"`
131		AttemptID    string `json:"attempt_id"`
132		BaseRev      string `json:"base_rev"`
133		ResultRev    string `json:"result_rev"`
134	}

136	const candidateChecksFailedEventKind = "candidate-checks-failed"
137	const candidateReviewClassifiedEventKind = "candidate-review-classified"
138	const repairCycleStartedEventKind = "repair-cycle-started"
139	
140	// CandidateChecksFailedEventBody is the structured payload of a
141	// candidate-checks-failed bead event.
142	type CandidateChecksFailedEventBody struct {
143		CandidateRef string   `json:"candidate_ref,omitempty"`
144		CycleIndex   int      `json:"cycle_index"`
145		AttemptID    string   `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
146		BaseRev      string   `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
147		ResultRev    string   `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
148		Detail       string   `json:"detail,omitempty"`
149		Artifacts    []string `json:"artifacts,omitempty"`
150	}
151	
152	// CandidateReviewClassifiedEventBody is the structured payload of a
153	// candidate-review-classified bead event.
154	type CandidateReviewClassifiedEventBody struct {
155		CandidateRef   string `json:"candidate_ref,omitempty"`
156		CycleIndex     int    `json:"cycle_index"`
157		AttemptID      string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
158		BaseRev        string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
159		ResultRev      string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
160		Verdict        string `json:"verdict,omitempty"`
161		Classification string `json:"classification,omitempty"`
162		Reason         string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
163	}
164	
165	// RepairCycleStartedEventBody is the structured payload of a
166	// repair-cycle-started bead event.
167	type RepairCycleStartedEventBody struct {
168		AttemptID          string `json:"attempt_id,omitempty"`
169		BaseRev            string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
170		FailedCandidateRev string `json:"failed_candidate_rev,omitempty"`
171		RepairCycleIndex   int    `json:"repair_cycle_index"`
172		Classification     string `json:"classification,omitempty"`
173	}
174	
175	// ShouldRetainCandidateRef returns true when the attempt outcome requires
176	// keeping the candidate ref for operator inspection and recovery.
177	// Only a successfully landed (merged) candidate allows the temporary ref to be
178	// cleaned up; preserved, conflicted, parked, and manual candidates retain it.
179	func ShouldRetainCandidateRef(status string) bool {
180		return status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
181	}
182	
183	// AttemptCycleResult is the outcome of AttemptCycleCoordinator.Run.
184	type AttemptCycleResult struct {
185		// Report is the final execute-bead report after all cycle phases complete.
186		Report ExecuteBeadReport
187		// Landed is true when the candidate was merged to the main branch.
188		Landed bool
189	}
190	
191	// AttemptCycleCoordinator owns the full live-worktree lifecycle for a single
192	// implementation attempt: implementation → optional checks → optional review →
193	// optional repair → land. It ensures terminal disposition is reached before
194	// the coordinator exits, regardless of which phase terminates the cycle.
195	//
196	// Pass and Lander are required. Nil optional fields (Checks, Reviewer, Repair,
197	// RefStore) skip their respective phases.
198	//
199	// Repair loops are reserved for future implementation. When Reviewer is non-nil
200	// the coordinator runs one pre-land candidate review before landing.
201	type AttemptCycleCoordinator struct {
202		Pass        ImplementationPass
203		Checks      CandidateCheckRunner // nil → skip pre-land checks
204		Reviewer    CandidateReviewer    // nil → skip candidate review (future)
205		Repair      RepairPass           // nil → skip repair loops (future)
206		Lander      CandidateLander
207		RefStore    CandidateRefStore // nil → no ref pinning
208		ProjectRoot string            // required when RefStore non-nil
209		BeadEvents  BeadEventAppender // nil → no candidate-cycle event emission
210		// RepairMaxCycles caps append-only repair attempts after review_fixable_gap.
211		// Values <=0 default to one repair cycle.
212		RepairMaxCycles int
213	}
214	
215	// Run executes one attempt cycle: implementation → checks → land. Non-success
216	// statuses (no_changes, execution_failed, etc.) return without calling the
217	// lander.
218	// The caller is responsible for worktree cleanup after Run returns.
219	//
220	// When RefStore and ProjectRoot are set, Run pins a candidate ref under
221	// refs/ddx/iterations/<attempt-id>/<cycle-index> before checks and review.
222	// After a successful land the temporary ref is cleaned up; for preserved,
223	// conflicted, parked, or otherwise non-landed outcomes the ref is retained so
224	// operators can inspect and recover the candidate.
225	func (c *AttemptCycleCoordinator) Run(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (AttemptCycleResult, error) {
226		candidate, err := c.Pass.Execute(ctx, beadID)
227		if err != nil {
228			return AttemptCycleResult{Report: candidate.Report}, err
229		}
230	
231		if candidate.Report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
232			return AttemptCycleResult{Report: candidate.Report}, nil
233		}
234	
235		repairCycles := 0
236		var pinnedRefs []string
237		for {
238			c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
239				Active:       true,
240				Phase:        "candidate",
241				CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
242				CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
243			})
244			c.pinCandidateRef(beadID, &candidate)
245			pinnedRefs = appendUniqueString(pinnedRefs, candidate.Report.CandidateRef)
246			c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
247				Active:       true,
248				Phase:        "candidate_pinned",
249				CandidateRef: candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
250				CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
251				CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
252			})
253	
254			if c.Checks != nil {
255				c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
256					Active:       true,
257					Phase:        "checks",
258					CandidateRef: candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
259					CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
260					CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
261				})
262				checksResult, checksErr := c.Checks.RunChecks(ctx, c.ProjectRoot, candidate)
263				if checksErr != nil {
264					checksResult.Detail = checksErr.Error()
265				}
266				if checksErr != nil || !checksResult.Passed {
267					report := candidate.Report
268					if checksErr != nil {
269						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
270					} else {
271						report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
272					}
273					report.OutcomeReason = candidateChecksFailedEventKind
274					report.Detail = candidateChecksFailedDetail(checksResult.Detail)
275					if checksErr != nil && report.Error == "" {
276						report.Error = checksErr.Error()
277					}
278					c.appendCandidateChecksFailedEvent(beadID, report, checksResult)
279					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
280				}
281			}
282	
283			if c.Reviewer == nil {
284				break
285			}
286	
287			c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
288				Active:       true,
289				Phase:        "review",
290				CandidateRef: candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
291				CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
292				CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
293				ReviewActive: true,
294			})
295			reviewResult, reviewErr := c.Reviewer.Review(ctx, c.ProjectRoot, candidate)
296			if reviewErr != nil {
297				report := candidate.Report
298				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
299				report.Detail = "pre-land review: " + reviewErr.Error()
300				report.ReviewVerdict = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Verdict)
301				report.ReviewRationale = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Rationale)
302				c.appendCandidateReviewClassifiedEvent(beadID, report, reviewResult, ReviewFindingClassMalfunction, reviewErr.Error())
303				return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
304			}
305	
306			verdict := Verdict(strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Verdict))
307			switch verdict {
308			case VerdictApprove:
309				candidate.Report.ReviewVerdict = string(VerdictApprove)
310				candidate.Report.ReviewRationale = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Rationale)
311				break
312			case VerdictRequestChanges, VerdictBlock:
313				classification := c.classifyCandidateReview(reviewResult)
314				report := candidate.Report
315				report.ReviewVerdict = string(verdict)
316				report.ReviewRationale = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Rationale)
317				if verdict == VerdictRequestChanges {
318					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges
319					report.Detail = "pre-land review: REQUEST_CHANGES"
320				} else {
321					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock
322					report.Detail = "pre-land review: BLOCK"
323				}
324				if verdict == VerdictBlock && classification.Class == ReviewFindingClassMalfunction {
325					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
326					report.Detail = "pre-land review: " + ReviewFindingClassMalfunction
327				}
328				c.appendCandidateReviewClassifiedEvent(beadID, report, reviewResult, classification.Class, classification.Reason)
329				if !classification.AutomatedRepairEligible || c.Repair == nil {
330					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
331				}
332				if repairCycles >= c.maxRepairCycles() {
333					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted
334					report.OutcomeReason = ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted
335					report.Detail = "pre-land repair: " + ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted
336					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
337				}
338				prompt := BuildRepairPrompt(repairPromptInput(beadID, candidate, reviewResult, repairCycles+1))
339				c.appendRepairCycleStartedEvent(beadID, candidate, repairCycles+1, classification.Class)
340				c.recordCandidateCycleState(candidate, CandidateCycleState{
341					Active:       true,
342					Phase:        "repair",
343					CandidateRef: candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
344					CandidateRev: candidate.Report.ResultRev,
345					CycleIndex:   candidate.CycleIndex,
346					RepairActive: true,
347				})
348				repaired, repairErr := c.Repair.Repair(ctx, candidate, prompt)
349				if repairErr != nil {
350					report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
351					report.Detail = "pre-land repair: " + repairErr.Error()
352					report.Error = repairErr.Error()
353					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
354				}
355				repairCycles++
356				candidate = normalizeRepairedCandidate(candidate, repaired)
357				if candidate.Report.Status != ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {
358					return AttemptCycleResult{Report: candidate.Report}, nil
359				}
360				continue
361			default:
362				report := candidate.Report
363				report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction
364				report.Detail = "pre-land review: malformed verdict"
365				report.ReviewVerdict = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Verdict)
366				report.ReviewRationale = strings.TrimSpace(reviewResult.Rationale)
367				c.appendCandidateReviewClassifiedEvent(beadID, report, reviewResult, ReviewFindingClassMalfunction, "malformed review verdict")
368				return AttemptCycleResult{Report: report}, nil
369			}
370			break
371		}
372	
373		landed, err := c.Lander.Land(ctx, candidate)
374		if err != nil {
375			return AttemptCycleResult{Report: candidate.Report}, err
376		}
377	
378		// Propagate candidate metadata to the landed report when the lander does
379		// not set them (e.g. in tests that return candidate.Report directly).
380		if landed.CandidateRef == "" && candidate.Report.CandidateRef != "" {
381			landed.CandidateRef = candidate.Report.CandidateRef
382			landed.CycleIndex = candidate.Report.CycleIndex
383		}
384	
385		result := AttemptCycleResult{Report: landed, Landed: true}
386	
387		// Clean up the candidate ref after successful landing. For any non-success
388		// outcome (preserved, conflicted, parked, manual) the ref is retained so
389		// operators can inspect the candidate commit after the worktree is removed.
390		if c.RefStore != nil && c.ProjectRoot != "" && landed.CandidateRef != "" {
391			if !ShouldRetainCandidateRef(landed.Status) {
392				for _, ref := range pinnedRefs {
393					_ = c.RefStore.UnpinCandidateRef(c.ProjectRoot, ref)
394				}
395			}
396		}
397	
398		return result, nil
399	}

536		}

582	// WriteWorktreeCandidateCycleState updates cleanup metadata and, when a
583	// matching run-state exists, the per-attempt run-state snapshot for a live
584	// candidate-cycle worktree.
585	func WriteWorktreeCandidateCycleState(projectRoot, wtPath, attemptID string, state CandidateCycleState) error {
586		meta, err := ReadExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath)
587		if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
588			return fmt.Errorf("write candidate cycle state: read metadata: %w", err)
589		}
590		meta.WorktreePath = firstNonEmpty(meta.WorktreePath, wtPath)
591		meta.ProjectRoot = firstNonEmpty(meta.ProjectRoot, projectRoot)
592		meta.AttemptID = firstNonEmpty(meta.AttemptID, attemptID)
593		applyCandidateCycleStateToMetadata(&meta, state)
594		if err := WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath, meta); err != nil {
595			return err
596		}
597		if projectRoot == "" || meta.AttemptID == "" {
598			return nil
599		}
600		states, err := ReadRunStates(projectRoot)
601		if err != nil {
602			return nil
603		}
604		for i := range states {
605			current := states[i]
606			if current.AttemptID != meta.AttemptID {
607				continue
608			}
609			applyCandidateCycleStateToRunState(&current, state)
610			return WriteRunState(projectRoot, current)
611		}
612		return nil
613	}

615	// MarkWorktreeActiveCycle sets the ActiveCandidateCycle flag in the worktree's
616	// cleanup metadata. The cleanup manager preserves any worktree with this flag
617	// set so it is never deleted mid-cycle before the coordinator disposes it.
618	func MarkWorktreeActiveCycle(wtPath string) error {
619		return WriteWorktreeCandidateCycleState("", wtPath, "", CandidateCycleState{
620			Active: true,
621			Phase:  "active",
622		})
623	}

625	// ClearWorktreeActiveCycle clears the ActiveCandidateCycle flag in the
626	// worktree's cleanup metadata after the coordinator reaches terminal disposition.
627	func ClearWorktreeActiveCycle(wtPath string) error {
628		meta, err := ReadExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath)
629		if err != nil {
630			if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
631				return nil
632			}
633			return fmt.Errorf("clear active cycle: read metadata: %w", err)
634		}
635		meta.ActiveCandidateCycle = false
636		meta.CandidateCyclePhase = ""
637		meta.ReviewActive = false
638		meta.RepairActive = false
639		return WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(wtPath, meta)
640	}

642	func applyCandidateCycleStateToMetadata(meta *ExecutionCleanupMetadata, state CandidateCycleState) {
643		meta.ActiveCandidateCycle = state.Active
644		meta.CandidateCyclePhase = state.Phase
645		meta.CandidateRef = state.CandidateRef
646		meta.CandidateRev = state.CandidateRev
647		meta.CycleIndex = state.CycleIndex
648		meta.ReviewActive = state.ReviewActive
649		meta.RepairActive = state.RepairActive
650	}

653		runState.CandidateCyclePhase = state.Phase

655		runState.CandidateRev = state.CandidateRev
656		runState.CycleIndex = state.CycleIndex
657		runState.ReviewActive = state.ReviewActive
658		runState.RepairActive = state.RepairActive

3	import (

7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9	)
10	
11	// ReviewFixableGapEventKind is the event kind emitted when a BLOCK verdict is
12	// classified as a fixable gap — an automated repair cycle is scheduled instead
13	// of routing to a terminal operator-required state.
14	const ReviewFixableGapEventKind = "review-fixable-gap"
15	
16	// RepairContextFromReviewGroup carries the review-group reference needed to
17	// schedule one bounded repair attempt after a review_fixable_gap verdict. The
18	// caller is responsible for computing the repair MinPower from
19	// ImplementerActualPower using the escalation ladder and for invoking the
20	// repair executor with the original harness/model/provider/profile pins
21	// unchanged.
22	type RepairContextFromReviewGroup struct {
23		// ReviewGroupID is the shared bundle ID of the review group whose BLOCK
24		// triggered the repair. Empty when the reviewer is a single-slot reviewer
25		// rather than a full review group.
26		ReviewGroupID string
27		// ResultRev is the result revision that the reviewer blocked. One repair
28		// cycle is permitted per ResultRev; a second review_fixable_gap on the
29		// same ResultRev falls through to the regular BLOCK triage path.
30		ResultRev string
31		// ReviewRationale is the reviewer's actionable rationale forwarded to the
32		// repair executor as context.
33		ReviewRationale string
34		// ImplementerActualPower is the routing power of the model that produced
35		// the blocked result. The repair attempt must use a MinPower strictly
36		// above this value.
37		ImplementerActualPower int
38	}
39	
40	// reviewFixableGapEventBody builds the structured body for a
41	// review-fixable-gap event.
42	func reviewFixableGapEventBody(groupID, resultRev, rationale string) string {
43		b := fmt.Sprintf("review_group_id=%s\nresult_rev=%s", groupID, resultRev)
44		if rationale != "" {
45			b += "\n" + rationale
46		}
47		return b
48	}
49	
50	// hasReviewFixableGapRepairScheduled reports whether a repair cycle has
51	// already been recorded for the given resultRev on this bead. Used to enforce
52	// the one-cycle-per-result limit: a second review_fixable_gap on the same
53	// resultRev falls through to the regular BLOCK triage path instead of
54	// scheduling another repair.
55	func hasReviewFixableGapRepairScheduled(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, resultRev string) bool {
56		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
57		if err != nil {
58			return false
59		}
60		needle := "result_rev=" + resultRev
61		for _, ev := range events {
62			if ev.Kind == ReviewFixableGapEventKind && strings.Contains(ev.Body, needle) {
63				return true
64			}
65		}
66		return false
67	}
68	
69	// scheduleReviewFixableGapRepair records a review-fixable-gap event on the
70	// bead and returns the RepairContextFromReviewGroup the caller needs to run
71	// the repair attempt. Callers must check hasReviewFixableGapRepairScheduled
72	// before calling this function to enforce the one-cycle limit.
73	func scheduleReviewFixableGapRepair(
74		store ExecuteBeadLoopStore,
75		beadID, actor string,
76		now time.Time,
77		reviewGroupID, resultRev, rationale string,
78		implActualPower int,
79	) *RepairContextFromReviewGroup {
80		_ = store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
81			Kind:      ReviewFixableGapEventKind,
82			Summary:   "review_fixable_gap",
83			Body:      reviewFixableGapEventBody(reviewGroupID, resultRev, rationale),
84			Actor:     actor,
85			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
86			CreatedAt: now.UTC(),
87		})
88		return &RepairContextFromReviewGroup{
89			ReviewGroupID:          reviewGroupID,
90			ResultRev:              resultRev,
91			ReviewRationale:        rationale,
92			ImplementerActualPower: implActualPower,
93		}
94	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import "strings"
4	
5	// OperatorCancelReason marks a landing preserved because the operator
6	// cancelled the attempt mid-flight via /api/beads/<id>/cancel. ADR-022
7	// §Cancel SLA: the worker aborts at the next safe point (between LLM turns /
8	// git ops) and emits a preserved_for_review result with this exact reason.
9	const OperatorCancelReason = "operator_cancel"
10	
11	// PushFailedReasonPrefix is the canonical Reason prefix written by the
12	// landing layer when `git push` rejects the new tip after a local merge or
13	// fast-forward succeeded. ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus matches on this prefix
14	// to map a merged-locally-but-not-pushed outcome to push_failed instead of
15	// success, so the bead stays open.
16	const PushFailedReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push failed:"
17	
18	// PushConflictReasonPrefix marks a more specific failure mode than
19	// PushFailedReasonPrefix: the local push was rejected because the remote
20	// advanced, the loop attempted an automatic pull/merge to recover, and the
21	// merge produced a conflict the loop cannot resolve. The bead is parked for
22	// human review with a short cooldown rather than a year-long park.
23	const PushConflictReasonPrefix = "landed locally; push conflict:"
24	
25	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
26	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
27	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
28	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
29	const (
30		FailureModeContextOverflow                = "context_overflow"
31		FailureModeMergeConflict                  = "merge_conflict"
32		FailureModeTestFailure                    = "test_failure"
33		FailureModeBuildFailure                   = "build_failure"
34		FailureModeTimeout                        = "timeout"
35		FailureModeAuthError                      = "auth_error"
36		FailureModeNoChanges                      = "no_changes"
37		FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced             = "no_evidence_produced"
38		FailureModeRatchetMiss                    = "ratchet_miss"
39		FailureModeNoViableProvider               = "no_viable_provider"
40		FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled            = "harness_not_installed"
41		FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint = "blocked_by_passthrough_constraint"
42		FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied          = "agent_power_unsatisfied"
43		FailureModeLockContention                 = "lock_contention"
44		FailureModeWorktreeLost                   = "worktree_lost"
45		FailureModeUnknown                        = "unknown"
46	)
47	
48	// ResourceExhaustedStopMessage is the operator-visible message emitted when
49	// execution must stop because the host cannot safely continue draining the
50	// queue.
51	const ResourceExhaustedStopMessage = "resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop"
52	
53	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
54	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
55	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
56	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
57	//
58	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
59	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
60	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
61	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
62	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
63	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
64		switch outcome {
65		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
66			if exitCode == 0 {
67				return ""
68			}
69			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
70			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
71		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
72			return FailureModeNoChanges
73		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
74			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
75		}
76	
77		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
78		switch {
79		case IsLockContentionError(errMsg):
80			return FailureModeLockContention
81		case IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg):
82			return FailureModeWorktreeLost
83		case containsAny(lower,
84			"passthrough constraint unsatisfiable",
85			"passthrough constraint:",
86			"max_power is less than min_power",
87			"max_power exceeds min_power",
88			"must be less than max_power",
89			"no viable routing candidate for pins",
90			"harness cannot satisfy power constraint",
91			"harness pin incompatible with power bounds",
92			"model pin incompatible with power bounds",
93			"provider pin incompatible with power bounds"):
94			return FailureModeBlockedByPassthroughConstraint
95		case containsAny(lower,
96			"agent power unsatisfied",
97			"no model meets min_power",
98			"no model with power >=",
99			"minimum power not achievable",
100			"min_power constraint cannot be satisfied"):
101			return FailureModeAgentPowerUnsatisfied
102		case containsAny(lower,
103			"executable file not found in $path",
104			"executable file not found",
105			"no such file or directory") &&
106			containsAny(lower, "exec:", "harness", "claude", "agent", "codex", "gemini"):
107			return FailureModeHarnessNotInstalled
108		case containsAny(lower,
109			"no viable harness",
110			"no harness configured",
111			"failed to initialize routing service",
112			"resolveroute:",
113			"no viable routing candidate",
114			"no live provider supports",
115			"no candidate satisfying local endpoint",
116			"no viable provider"):
117			return FailureModeNoViableProvider
118		case containsAny(lower,
119			"context length",
120			"context_length_exceeded",
121			"context window",
122			"maximum context",
123			"prompt is too long",
124			"prompt too long",
125			"token limit",
126			"too many tokens",
127			"exceeds the context"):
128			return FailureModeContextOverflow
129		case containsAny(lower,
130			"unauthorized",
131			"401 unauthorized",
132			"invalid api key",
133			"invalid_api_key",
134			"authentication failed",
135			"authentication_error",
136			"no api key",
137			"missing api key",
138			"quota exceeded",
139			"insufficient quota",
140			"insufficient_quota",
141			"rate limit",
142			"rate_limit",
143			"429"):
144			return FailureModeAuthError
145		case containsAny(lower,
146			"timeout",
147			"timed out",
148			"context deadline exceeded",
149			"deadline exceeded",
150			"signal: killed"):
151			return FailureModeTimeout
152		case containsAny(lower,
153			"merge conflict",
154			"ff-merge not possible",
155			"merge failed",
156			"automatic merge failed"):
157			return FailureModeMergeConflict
158		case containsAny(lower,
159			"build failed",
160			"compilation failed",
161			"compile error",
162			"compilation error",
163			"cannot find package",
164			"undefined:",
165			"undefined reference",
166			"syntax error"):
167			return FailureModeBuildFailure
168		case containsAny(lower,
169			"test failed",
170			"tests failed",
171			"--- fail:",
172			"assertion failed",
173			"test failure"):
174			return FailureModeTestFailure
175		}
176		if outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed && errMsg != "" {
177			return FailureModeUnknown
178		}
179		if exitCode != 0 {
180			return FailureModeUnknown
181		}
182		return ""
183	}
184	
185	// IsWorktreeLostError reports whether errMsg describes the execute-bead
186	// infrastructure losing the isolated attempt worktree before DDx could inspect
187	// its HEAD. This deliberately requires both a HEAD/worktree signal and a
188	// missing-path signal so generic harness "no such file" errors do not get
189	// bucketed as infrastructure loss.
190	func IsWorktreeLostError(errMsg string) bool {
191		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
192		hasHeadSignal := containsAny(lower,
193			"failed to read worktree head",
194			"git rev-parse head",
195			"worktree path missing")
196		hasMissingPathSignal := containsAny(lower,
197			"chdir",
198			"no such file or directory",
199			"cannot access",
200			"does not exist")
201		return hasHeadSignal && hasMissingPathSignal
202	}

204	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
205	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
206	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
207		for _, n := range needles {
208			if n == "" {
209				continue
210			}
211			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
212				return true
213			}
214		}
215		return false
216	}

218	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
219	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
220	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
221	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
222	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
223	// bead merged cleanly.
224	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
225		switch landingOutcome {
226		case "merged":
227			return ""
228		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
229			return FailureModeNoChanges
230		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
231			return FailureModeNoEvidenceProduced
232		case "error":
233			if workerMode != "" {
234				return workerMode
235			}
236			return FailureModeUnknown
237		case "preserved":
238			switch {
239			case landingReason == "merge conflict" || landingReason == "merge failed" || landingReason == "ff-merge not possible":
240				return FailureModeMergeConflict
241			case landingReason == "post-run checks failed", strings.HasPrefix(landingReason, PreMergeChecksReason):
242				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
243			case landingReason == RatchetPreserveReason:
244				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
245			case landingReason == OperatorCancelReason:
246				return ""
247			}
248			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
249			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
250			if workerMode != "" {
251				return workerMode
252			}
253			return FailureModeUnknown
254		}
255		if workerMode != "" {
256			return workerMode
257		}
258		return FailureModeUnknown
259	}

261	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
262	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
263	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
264	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
265	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
266	// test-oriented by convention.
267	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
268		for _, gr := range gateResults {
269			if gr.Status != "fail" {
270				continue
271			}
272			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
273			if containsAny(combined,
274				"build failed",
275				"compilation failed",
276				"compile error",
277				"compilation error",
278				"cannot find package",
279				"undefined:",
280				"undefined reference",
281				"syntax error") {
282				return FailureModeBuildFailure
283			}
284		}
285		return FailureModeTestFailure
286	}

288	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
289	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
290	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
291	const (
292		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded  = "task_succeeded"
293		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed     = "task_failed"
294		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges  = "task_no_changes"
295		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence = "task_no_evidence"
296	)
297	
298	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
299	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
300	const (
301		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
302		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
303		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
304		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
305		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
306		ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed                 = "push_failed"
307		ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict               = "push_conflict"
308		ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview       = "preserved_needs_review"
309		ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced         = "no_evidence_produced"
310		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
311		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
312		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
313		ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted          = "resource_exhausted"
314	
315		// ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition is a structured outcome
316		// signalling that the executor inspected the bead and concluded it cannot
317		// be delivered in a single pass — typically because the scope is
318		// epic-sized and must first be broken into sub-beads. It is distinct
319		// from no_changes (which means "I tried and there was nothing to change")
320		// and from execution_failed (which means the agent or harness errored).
321		//
322		// The loop treats this status as terminal-for-loop: it parks the bead
323		// with a long cooldown and records the recommended sub-beads as a
324		// structured `decomposition-recommendation` event. No further attempts
325		// happen until an operator clears the cooldown (e.g. via
326		// `ddx bead cooldown clear`) or splits the bead.
327		ExecuteBeadStatusDeclinedNeedsDecomposition = "declined_needs_decomposition"
328	
329		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
330		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
331		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
332		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
333		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
334	
335		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable is set when execute-loop
336		// attempted 3-way ort auto-resolution and (if configured) a focused
337		// conflict-resolve agent run for a preserved iteration, and both failed.
338		// The bead is parked under LandConflictCooldown for operator review.
339		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable = "land_conflict_unresolvable"
340	
341		// ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired is set when the focused
342		// conflict-resolve agent escalated to BLOCKING — the conflict requires
343		// operator judgment, not automated retry. The bead moves to status=proposed
344		// with a kind:land-conflict-operator-required event.
345		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictOperatorRequired = "land_conflict_operator_required"
346	
347		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock is set when a BLOCK review verdict
348		// is classified as a terminal operator-required condition —
349		// review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth
350		// cap, or review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope. These classes do not re-enter
351		// the automated retry cycle and do not consume the no-progress retry
352		// budget; the bead moves to status=proposed until an operator
353		// intervenes.
354		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewTerminalBlock = "review_terminal_block"
355	
356		// ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap is set when a BLOCK review verdict is
357		// classified as a fixable gap — the issues are addressable by a repair
358		// attempt at higher MinPower with the reviewer's rationale as context.
359		// Exactly one repair cycle is scheduled per result_rev; a second
360		// review_fixable_gap on the same result_rev falls through to the regular
361		// BLOCK triage path instead.
362		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewFixableGap = "review_fixable_gap"
363	
364		// ExecuteBeadStatusRepairCycleExhausted is set when the candidate-cycle
365		// repair loop reaches its per-attempt repair_max_cycles limit before a

370	
371	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
372	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
373	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
374	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
375		if exitCode != 0 {
376			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
377		}
378	
379		switch outcome {
380		case "merged":
381			// A merged outcome whose reason carries the push-failure marker
382			// means the local target branch advanced but `git push` rejected the
383			// new tip (large blob, branch protection, auth, divergence, etc.).
384			// Classify these as push_failed so the loop refuses to close the
385			// bead and operators are forced to investigate.
386			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushConflictReasonPrefix) {
387				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushConflict
388			}
389			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, PushFailedReasonPrefix) {
390				return ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed
391			}
392			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
393		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
394			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
395		case "no-evidence", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoEvidence:
396			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced
397		case "error":
398			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
399		case "preserved":
400			switch {
401			case reason == "merge conflict" || reason == "merge failed" || reason == "ff-merge not possible":
402				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
403			case reason == "post-run checks failed", strings.HasPrefix(reason, PreMergeChecksReason):
404				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
405			case reason == RatchetPreserveReason:
406				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
407			case reason == OperatorCancelReason:
408				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
409			}
410			if isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason) {
411				return ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview
412			}
413			// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
414			// explicitly requested no merge.
415			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
416		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
417			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
418		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
419			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
420		default:
421			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
422		}
423	}

426	// host-resource failure classification.
427	func IsResourceExhaustedStatus(status string) bool {
428		return status == ExecuteBeadStatusResourceExhausted
429	}
430	
431	func isPreservedNeedsReviewReason(reason string) bool {
432		for _, prefix := range []string{
433			"evidence commit failed:",
434			"large-deletion gate:",
435			"syntax sanity gate:",
436			"post-land gate failed:",
437		} {
438			if strings.HasPrefix(reason, prefix) {
439				return true
440			}
441		}
442		return false
443	}

445	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
446		switch {
447		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
448			return reason + ": " + errMsg
449		case reason != "":
450			return reason
451		case errMsg != "":
452			return errMsg
453		case status != "":
454			return status
455		default:
456			return "unknown"
457		}
458	}
